name: Sarah Chen
notes: Co-founder. Sets company strategy and owns investor relationships. Drives the Series C narrative. Increasingly focused on AI transformation story as a competitive differentiator.

name: Marcus Webb
notes: Co-founder. Owns the technical roadmap and engineering org. Championed the tech debt freeze in Q1 2026. Skeptical of AI hype but open to specific, high-ROI use cases.

name: Priya Sharma
notes: Manages a team of 8 CSMs. Deeply frustrated by the broken public roadmap commitment from Product. Sits on the Customer Advisory Board with top-tier accounts.

name: Tom Adeyemi
notes: Owns product strategy and the roadmap. Was the primary skeptic of moving to Linear from Jira. Now a convert. Currently navigating tension between tech debt freeze and customer commitments.

name: Yuki Tanaka
notes: Runs day-to-day engineering operations. Manages 22 engineers across 3 squads. Champions the engineering runbook library initiative. Was the loudest voice for the tech debt freeze.

name: Nadia Kovalchuk
notes: Owns month-end close and financial reporting to the board. Spends an estimated 40 hours per month on manual reconciliation. Primary internal champion for AI in finance workflows.

name: James Okafor
notes: Technical lead for the Finance Workflow Engine — Veridian's highest-revenue module. Point person for the Hartfield SSO integration. Raised concerns internally about the commitment timeline before it was made.

name: Fatima Al-Rashid
notes: Joined 18 months ago. Hired externally — controversial decision that bypassed two internal candidates. Now owns cross-functional ops, including the commitment tracking problem Veridian has quietly ignored.

name: Chloe Beaumont
notes: Directly manages the Hartfield Group and Meridian Capital accounts. Delivered the consolidated bug report format that Engineering requested. Informally owns institutional knowledge about customer commitments — keeps a personal Notion doc tracking them.

name: Ravi Patel
notes: Owns new business and expansion revenue. Was the dissenting voice when Veridian chose Salesforce over HubSpot — preferred HubSpot for its easier integration with Outreach. Now an unenthusiastic Salesforce power user.

name: Dev Anand
notes: Owns the Customer Data Platform. Built the Looker dashboards used by CS and Sales. Sits at the intersection of Engineering and the AI readiness conversation — understands data quality issues better than anyone.

name: Anya Volkov
notes: Owns HR, recruiting, and people ops. Key stakeholder in the COO hiring decision. Runs the quarterly planning process that surfaces cross-functional commitments — but currently has no system to track them after the meeting.
